LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, AND, MATTHEW ROTHSCHILD MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECTS CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. THE CITY HAS IMMUNITY PURSUANT TO GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 815(A). EVEN WITHOUT IMMUNITY PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE CITY OWED HIM A DUTY TO IDENTIFY THE DOG AND OWNER WITH DUE CARE. IT WAS NOT FORESEEABLE THAT THE FAILURE TO LOCATE THE VIDEO WOULD RESULT IN PLAINTIFF HAVING TO GET RABIES SHOTS. PLAINTIFF HAD TO GET RABIES SHOTS BECAUSE HE WAS BITTEN BY A DOG, NOT BECAUSE MUNI FAILED TO LOCATE A VIDEO. TOO MANY ASSUMPTIONS ARE MADE TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION, SUCH AS THE LOCATION OF THE VIDEO WOULD HAVE LED TO THE IDENTIFICATION OF THE DOG WITHIN TEN DAYS AND THAT THE DOG DID NOT HAVE RABIES. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT ROTHSCHILD OWED HIM A DUTY. IT IS NOT FORESEEABLE THAT AN INACCURATE EMAIL BY ROTHSCHILD WOULD CAUSE PLAINTIFF TO HAVE TO GET RABIES SHOTS. AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION, NO SHOWING THAT ANY ACT OR OMISSION BY CCSF OR ANY OF ITS EMPLOYEES WAS THE PROXIMATE CAUSE OF PLAINTIFF'S DAMAGES (RABIES SHOTS). (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KATY ANNE BERSAMIN, CSR #11722

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and, MATTHEW ROTHSCHILD Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action granted without leave to amend, no reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. The City has immunity pursuant to Government Code section 815(a). Even without immunity plaintiff has not established that the City owed him a duty to identify the dog and owner with due care. It was not foreseeable that the failure to locate the video would result in plaintiff having to get rabies shots. Plaintiff had to get rabies shots because he was bitten by a dog, not because MUNI failed to locate a video. Too many assumptions are made to support a cause of action, such as the location of the video would have led to the identification of the dog within ten days and that the dog did not have rabies. Plaintiff fails to establish that Rothschild owed him a duty. It is not foreseeable that an inaccurate email by Rothschild would cause plaintiff to have to get rabies shots. As to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action, no showing that any act or omission by CCSF or any of its employees was the proximate cause of plaintiff's damages (rabies shots). (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Katy Anne Bersamin, CSR # 11722

Special Motion To Strike Slapp Suit
Off Calendar Dec-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, and, MATTHEW ROTHSCHILD Special Motion to Strike SLAPP Suit. Appearances: Plaintiff Jeff Breibart, appearing in Pro Per and opposing party; Joshua S. White, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-4259 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Defendant fails to make a prima facie showing of protected activity. No showing e-mail to MUNI concerned an issue under consideration by a public entity. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
